description: A working log for research taste: each row starts with a problem, names the prior art, makes a forecast, defines what would falsify it, and points to the cheapest next test.

items.problem: Prediction-market APIs are improving, but professional flow still lacks unified collateral, margin, custody, settlement, and cross-venue risk views.
items.why_now: Kalshi, Polymarket, ForecastEx, IBKR, PMXT, and venue aggregators are making the trading surface legible; the capital layer is now the bottleneck.
prior_art: Crypto followed the same path: CCXT unified APIs before FalconX, Hidden Road, and Coinbase Prime professionalized the capital layer.
Options became one market only after OCC-style clearing made contracts fungible across venues.
items.forecast: The first durable wedge is funded-account orchestration plus position, exposure, and resolution-risk monitoring, not full cross-venue clearing.
items.falsifier: Funds say venue-level accounts are fine, or the largest venues ship native portfolio margin and cross-venue reporting before independents gain trust.
items.cheap_test: Interview 10 prediction-market desks and ask what they reconcile daily by spreadsheet: balances, margin, resolution criteria, or venue limits.
items.belief_update: Strong until disproven by buyer calls.
items.public_artifact: Atlas opportunity note and target outreach list.

items.problem: CMBS analysis requires deal waterfalls, loan-level IRP data, property fundamentals, and market pricing, but the workflow remains expensive and specialist-heavy.
items.why_now: CRE stress and refinancing walls make loan-level collateral truth more valuable, while public EDGAR and trustee data are still messy enough to reward normalization.
prior_art: Trepp, CRED iQ, Bloomberg, KBRA, Moody's CRE, CoStar, MSCI-RCA, and Green Street cover pieces of the stack.
items.forecast: A narrow public-data teardown for one real deal can expose enough edge to support paid collateral surveillance memos.
items.falsifier: The free filings are too incomplete to produce useful property-level variance, or every useful field requires enterprise licenses.
items.cheap_test: Pick one recent public CMBS deal, parse the prospectus and one IRP file, then reconcile top-10 loan DSCR and occupancy against property-market comps.
items.belief_update: Worth one deal before any product build.

items.problem: Crypto execution is not one market: routers, AMMs, perp venues, prime brokers, custody networks, and data surfaces are different adapter shapes.
items.why_now: The atlas had only Talos in Crypto Infrastructure × L2, which hid the institutional execution and prime-brokerage layer.
prior_art: Talos and CoinRoutes are OEMS/SOR layers.
FalconX, Coinbase Prime, Hidden Road, Copper ClearLoop, and Fireblocks bridge execution with custody, financing, and settlement.
items.forecast: The best map is not a flat DEX list; it separates quote routers, pool protocols, perp venues, custody networks, and prime brokerage.
items.falsifier: Users cannot explain decisions better with adapter shapes than with ordinary company categories.
items.cheap_test: Use the updated matrix to classify 20 more crypto venues without adding any taxonomy keys.
items.result: Crypto-Native Infrastructure × L2 moved from 1 to 7 entries.
items.belief_update: Framework improved; still needs lending-rate adapters and router/pool coverage.

items.problem: Parcel, tax delinquency, title/lien, permits/code, crime, CMBS exposure, and local market comps are fragmented across public agencies and vendors.
items.why_now: Investors and operators want hyperlocal risk screens, but most products stop at parcel or listing data instead of area-level joined decision data.
prior_art: Reonomy, LightBox, Cherre, CompStak, CoStar, Crexi, and county assessor systems each cover fragments.
CMBS workflows prove the value of linking asset-level facts to financing structures.
items.forecast: The wedge is not another property database; it is county-level anomaly and diligence packs around distressed or financeable areas.
items.falsifier: County data quality and rights make automated joining too brittle outside a few pilot counties.
items.cheap_test: Build one county pack by hand for parcel + delinquency + liens + permits + crime around 50 properties and measure how many records reconcile cleanly.
items.belief_update: High friction, but good research artifact if scoped county-by-county.

items.problem: Does cross-sectional commodity carry survive once you remove the roll artifact in continuous futures?
items.why_now: Most backtests run carry on raw continuous prices, where the roll splice quietly rewards contango.
items.forecast: The Sharpe-2 result is a free-parameter artifact; held-contract returns collapse it to zero.
items.falsifier: A back-adjusted, held-contract test with no threshold still shows Sharpe above 1.
items.cheap_test: Buy the full per-contract curve, key on instrument_id, run carry on held-contract returns.
items.result: Killed. The Sharpe 2.05 was a cleaning threshold set monotonically by one knob. Back-adjusted truth: -0.17, equal to placebo. TS-momentum held at +0.32 on the same returns.
items.belief_update: Carry is decayed, not a false negative. Never test futures carry on raw continuous prices.
items.public_artifact: Method note: held-contract returns as the only knob-free carry test.

items.problem: Is there a tradeable edge in daily perp positioning (leverage dispersion, whale-vs-retail tilt, OI change)?
items.why_now: Per-account snapshots across 24 coins look like they should predict next-day returns.
prior_art: Deflated Sharpe and multiple-testing corrections, Bailey and Lopez de Prado.
items.forecast: At least one signal clears Fama-MacBeth t above 3 after size and vol controls.
items.falsifier: Nothing clears t above 3 and a shuffled best-of-N placebo matches the best real signal.
items.cheap_test: 14-signal library as long-short terciles, gated by FM-t, Deflated Sharpe, and a shuffle placebo.
items.result: Killed. Best real signal t near 1.7; the placebo's best-of-14 hit 1.99 by chance. The prior lead decayed train 2.65 to test 0.93.
items.belief_update: No edge in static daily positioning. Any edge lives in intraday flow. The engine working is it rejecting noise.
items.public_artifact: Signal-discovery engine: mechanism-first, multiple-testing corrected, placebo-gated.

items.problem: Order-flow imbalance predicts next-bar reversion; is it harvestable net of fees?
items.why_now: Tick fills make per-coin OFI computable, and the in-sample t was around -6 at one minute.
prior_art: Cont, Kukanov, Stoikov, 'The price impact of order book events'.
items.forecast: Real signal, but per-bar size sits below the maker round-trip, so few coins clear.
items.falsifier: A meaningful share of coins stay net-positive after a realistic maker round-trip.
items.cheap_test: OFI deciles across 174 coins, entry at next bar, minus a 3 bp maker round-trip.
items.result: Killed. 0 of 174 coins net-positive. The t of -6 was intra-minute impact, gone by the next executable bar.
items.belief_update: Closes the OFI / maker-MM family. Significance at the print is not tradability at the next bar.
items.public_artifact: Execution-cost teardown of a real microstructure signal.

items.problem: Do forced-liquidation cascades create a next-day reversion that genuine selloffs do not?
items.why_now: Real per-account liquidation data lets you condition reversion on forced-flow fuel, not a price proxy.
prior_art: Fire-sale and forced-deleveraging literature, Shleifer and Vishny.
items.forecast: Conditioning on real liquidation fuel flips a losing price-only signal positive; the continuation placebo loses.
items.falsifier: Liquidation conditioning adds nothing over a naive price-drop signal, or fails Newey-West and subperiods.
items.cheap_test: Buy the big-down day only after a real liquidation cluster, exit next day, against a price-only proxy.
items.result: Inconclusive, open. Real fuel flipped Sharpe -2.48 to +1.23 and drawdown -78 to -28 percent, but NW-t 0.44, DSR 0.55, one bear year.
items.belief_update: Mechanism is real and the data is the difference-maker, but not yet significant. Needs intraday and more history.
items.public_artifact: Forced-flow conditioning study, real data versus proxy.
